# 🏋️ Training (196)

This is where raw weights become something brilliant. Whether you're fine-tuning a 7B on one GPU or pretraining across a cluster, you'll find the recipes, frameworks, and tricks that turn compute into capability.

[↑ Back to index](../README.md)

## Fine-tuning

Adapt foundation models to your task with LoRA, PEFT, and full-parameter tuning

<details>
<summary>Show 25 repos</summary>

- [karpathy/nanoGPT](https://github.com/karpathy/nanoGPT) — ⭐ 57,616 · `Python` · Framework — A simple and fast implementation for training and fine-tuning medium-sized GPT models, providing code and tools for practical generative pre-trained transformer development.
- [tloen/alpaca-lora](https://github.com/tloen/alpaca-lora) — ⭐ 18,923 · `Jupyter Notebook` · Paper Code — This repository provides code and instructions for instruct-tuning LLaMA models using LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation) on readily available consumer hardware, making large language model customization more accessible.
- [modelscope/ms-swift](https://github.com/modelscope/ms-swift) — ⭐ 14,279 · `Python` · Framework — MS-Swift is a comprehensive framework for efficiently fine-tuning (CPT/SFT/DPO/GRPO) a vast array of Large Language Models (LLMs) and Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) using techniques like PEFT or full-parameter training.
- [Lightning-AI/litgpt](https://github.com/Lightning-AI/litgpt) — ⭐ 13,387 · `Python` · Library — LitGPT offers recipes and implementations for over 20 high-performance Large Language Models. It provides tools and guidance for pretraining, fine-tuning, and deploying these models efficiently at scale.
- [ostris/ai-toolkit](https://github.com/ostris/ai-toolkit) — ⭐ 10,747 · `Python` · Library — This repository offers a comprehensive training toolkit specifically designed for fine-tuning diffusion models. It provides tools and utilities to streamline the process of adapting these generative models for specific image or video tasks.
- [oumi-ai/oumi](https://github.com/oumi-ai/oumi) — ⭐ 9,356 · `Python` · Framework — This repository provides a framework for easily fine-tuning, evaluating, and deploying various open-source Large Language Models (LLMs) and Vision-Language Models (VLMs). It supports models like Gemma, Qwen, and DeepSeek-R1, aiding in the complete lifecycle management of AI models.
- [meta-pytorch/torchtune](https://github.com/meta-pytorch/torchtune) — ⭐ 5,760 · `Python` · Library — Torchtune is a PyTorch-native library designed for post-training operations, primarily focused on fine-tuning and optimizing Large Language Models (LLMs).
- [PacktPublishing/LLM-Engineers-Handbook](https://github.com/PacktPublishing/LLM-Engineers-Handbook) — ⭐ 4,969 · `Python` · Tutorial/Course — A practical guide for LLM engineers covering fundamentals, fine-tuning, deployment of LLM and RAG applications to AWS, and LLMOps best practices.
- [h2oai/h2o-llmstudio](https://github.com/h2oai/h2o-llmstudio) — ⭐ 4,926 · `Python` · Framework — H2O LLM Studio is a Python framework with a no-code graphical user interface that facilitates the fine-tuning of large language models like Llama and GPT for various generative AI applications.
- [iusztinpaul/hands-on-llms](https://github.com/iusztinpaul/hands-on-llms) — ⭐ 3,415 · `Jupyter Notebook` · Tutorial/Course — This repository provides a comprehensive course on LLMs, LLM-Ops, and vector databases. It guides users through designing, training, and deploying a real-time financial advisor LLM system, including source code, videos, and reading materials.
- [NovaSky-AI/SkyThought](https://github.com/NovaSky-AI/SkyThought) — ⭐ 3,394 · `Python` · Application — This project allows users to train their own O1 preview models for a specified cost, focusing on practical AI/ML system building.
- [merveenoyan/smol-vision](https://github.com/merveenoyan/smol-vision) — ⭐ 1,921 · `Jupyter Notebook` · Tutorial/Course — This repository provides recipes and tutorials for shrinking, optimizing, and customizing cutting-edge vision models. It includes demos for real-time webcam processing, satellite imagery segmentation, and phone UI detection.
- [kohya-ss/musubi-tuner](https://github.com/kohya-ss/musubi-tuner) — ⭐ 1,864 · `Python` · Library — A Python library or CLI tool for fine-tuning AI models, likely focusing on diffusion models for image generation. It provides utilities for optimizing and training models efficiently.
- [Goekdeniz-Guelmez/mlx-lm-lora](https://github.com/Goekdeniz-Guelmez/mlx-lm-lora) — ⭐ 394 · `Python` · Library — A Python library for fine-tuning Large Language Models (LLMs) on Apple's MLX framework, featuring advanced techniques like LoRA, DPO, and memory-efficient training methods.
- [leochlon/ntkmirror](https://github.com/leochlon/ntkmirror) — ⭐ 293 · `Python` · Library — This project introduces ntkmirror, a novel fine-tuning method for large models that freezes the model weights and instead learns sparse signed log-gates on each layer's output to control activation magnitudes. It aims to adjust activation distributions with very low training costs, allowing controllers to be combined like vectors or stored.
- [rileycleavenger/Silicon-Studio](https://github.com/rileycleavenger/Silicon-Studio) — ⭐ 222 · `TypeScript` · Application — Silicon Studio is a desktop application that allows users to fine-tune and run large language models (LLMs) locally on Apple Silicon Macs. It provides a powerful interface built on Apple's MLX framework for a zero-setup AI experience.
- [Saivineeth147/lora-speedrun](https://github.com/Saivineeth147/lora-speedrun) — ⭐ 122 · `Python` · CLI Tool — This repository provides a system and public leaderboard for benchmarking the speed of LoRA and QLoRA fine-tuning for large language models, using a predefined task and hardware setup.
- [BuffaloBuffaloBuffaloBuffalo/ai-toolkit-perceptual](https://github.com/BuffaloBuffaloBuffaloBuffalo/ai-toolkit-perceptual) — ⭐ 83 · `Python` · Library — This Python toolkit extends the "AI Toolkit by Ostris" by adding perceptual anchoring and weight noising. It's designed to enhance LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation) training, primarily for fine-tuning models.
- [mkurman/grpo-llm-evaluator](https://github.com/mkurman/grpo-llm-evaluator) — ⭐ 54 · `Python` · Library — This repository implements GRPO to fine-tune a student LLM, leveraging teacher feedback to enhance its reasoning capabilities and overall answer quality through provided evaluations.
- [JuliusBrussee/cavegemma](https://github.com/JuliusBrussee/cavegemma) — ⭐ 39 · `Python` · CLI Tool — This repository provides the necessary code and configuration to apply a LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation) fine-tuning method to the Gemma 4 31B large language model, enabling it to generate text output in a distinct "caveman-mode" style. It's designed to customize the LLM's linguistic behavior for specific stylistic effects.
- [avbiswas/finetuning\_recipes](https://github.com/avbiswas/finetuning_recipes) — ⭐ 33 · `Python` · Boilerplate/Template — A collection of code recipes and examples for fine-tuning machine learning models, likely intended for educational or practical implementation as part of a training course.
- [Goekdeniz-Guelmez/MLX-LoRA-Studio](https://github.com/Goekdeniz-Guelmez/MLX-LoRA-Studio) — ⭐ 19 · Application — MLX-LoRA-Studio is a native macOS application designed for fine-tuning Large Language Models (LLMs) on Apple Silicon. It provides a user-friendly and customizable interface for on-device training.
- [nomadoor/Kura](https://github.com/nomadoor/Kura) — ⭐ 11 · `Python` · Application — Kura is a Python tool designed to simplify LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation) training, allowing users to provide a dataset and let an AI handle the fine-tuning process. It aims to make LoRA model creation more accessible and reduce the likelihood of training failures.
- [RL-MIND/OrthoReg](https://github.com/RL-MIND/OrthoReg) — ⭐ 10 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository provides the official Python code for OrthoReg, a method presented at CVPR 2026. It focuses on understanding and enforcing weight disentanglement in task arithmetic through orthogonal weight updates during fine-tuning to improve model performance.
- [gvkhosla/pi-tinker](https://github.com/gvkhosla/pi-tinker) — ⭐ 10 · `TypeScript` · CLI Tool — A TypeScript-based CLI tool designed for fine-tuning open-source models within the Pi environment, offering managed workflows for data preparation, evaluations, smoke tests, deployment, and chat checkpointing.

</details>

## RL/RLHF

Align models to human preferences with PPO, DPO, and reward modeling that actually converges

<details>
<summary>Show 65 repos</summary>

- [verl-project/verl](https://github.com/verl-project/verl) — ⭐ 21,591 · `Python` · Framework — This is a flexible and efficient framework for post-training reinforcement learning, focused on monitoring and improving the performance of RL models, particularly with OPD overlap diagnostics.
- [vwxyzjn/cleanrl](https://github.com/vwxyzjn/cleanrl) — ⭐ 9,690 · `Python` · Library — CleanRL provides high-quality, single-file implementations of popular Deep Reinforcement Learning (RL) algorithms such as PPO, DQN, and SAC. It is designed with research-friendly features for experimentation and learning in RL.
- [OpenRLHF/OpenRLHF](https://github.com/OpenRLHF/OpenRLHF) — ⭐ 9,552 · `Python` · Framework — This is an easy-to-use, scalable, and high-performance framework for Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF), designed for agentic AI and based on Ray, supporting various RL algorithms and VLM integration.
- [OpenPipe/ART](https://github.com/OpenPipe/ART) — ⭐ 9,483 · `Python` · Application — ART (Agent Reinforcement Trainer) is a Python tool that utilizes GRPO for "on-the-job training" of multi-step AI agents. It focuses on applying reinforcement learning to enhance agents' performance on real-world tasks, supporting various LLMs like Qwen3.6, GPT-OSS, and Llama.
- [huggingface/smol-course](https://github.com/huggingface/smol-course) — ⭐ 6,674 · `Jupyter Notebook` · Tutorial/Course — A course that teaches how to align small AI models, focusing on concepts relevant to machine learning, AI agents, and building real-world systems.
- [THUDM/slime](https://github.com/THUDM/slime) — ⭐ 5,818 · `Python` · Framework — Slime is an LLM post-training framework designed for Reinforcement Learning (RL) scaling. It helps optimize LLMs for long-horizon agent tasks by ensuring sampled trajectories align correctly with optimization targets.
- [areal-project/AReaL](https://github.com/areal-project/AReaL) — ⭐ 5,526 · `Python` · Framework — AReaL is an RL Bridge, a framework designed to simplify and make flexible the application of Reinforcement Learning to LLM-based agent applications. It proposes a new architecture for creating self-evolving agents that can learn continuously from live workloads.
- [inclusionAI/AReaL](https://github.com/inclusionAI/AReaL) — ⭐ 5,143 · `Python` · Framework — AReaL provides a scalable and flexible reinforcement learning (RL) infrastructure. It bridges foundation model training with modern LLM-based agent applications and simplifies the development of agentic AI systems.
- [huggingface/deep-rl-class](https://github.com/huggingface/deep-rl-class) — ⭐ 4,876 · `MDX` · Tutorial/Course — This repository hosts the official Hugging Face Deep Reinforcement Learning Course, providing educational materials and practical exercises for learning about deep reinforcement learning.
- [PrimeIntellect-ai/verifiers](https://github.com/PrimeIntellect-ai/verifiers) — ⭐ 4,295 · `Python` · Library — This repository offers a library for Reinforcement Learning (RL) environments and evaluation frameworks. It is intended for developing and testing RL agents within simulated environments, aligning with advanced LLM training methodologies.
- [NousResearch/hermes-agent-self-evolution](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent-self-evolution) — ⭐ 3,896 · `Python` · Agent Framework — This repository enables evolutionary self-improvement for AI agents like Hermes, focusing on optimizing their skills, prompts, and code using frameworks like DSPy and GEPA.
- [huggingface/OpenEnv](https://github.com/huggingface/OpenEnv) — ⭐ 1,936 · `Python` · Library — OpenEnv is an interface library designed for post-training reinforcement learning with various environments. It allows frontier labs to effectively train and harness their models.
- [NovaSky-AI/SkyRL](https://github.com/NovaSky-AI/SkyRL) — ⭐ 1,901 · `Python` · Library — SkyRL is a modular full-stack library for applying Reinforcement Learning (RL) techniques to Large Language Models (LLMs). It helps in the training and optimization of LLMs using RL methods.
- [meta-pytorch/OpenEnv](https://github.com/meta-pytorch/OpenEnv) — ⭐ 1,830 · `Python` · Library — This is an interface library for post-training work with Reinforcement Learning (RL) environments, providing a standardized way to interact with and test RL agents in simulated environments.
- [walkinglabs/hands-on-modern-rl](https://github.com/walkinglabs/hands-on-modern-rl) — ⭐ 1,727 · `Python` · Tutorial/Course — This is an open-source, hands-on curriculum that guides learners from basic Reinforcement Learning (RL) concepts to advanced topics like LLM alignment and agentic systems. It provides practical tutorials to build and understand modern AI agents.
- [FareedKhan-dev/all-rl-algorithms](https://github.com/FareedKhan-dev/all-rl-algorithms) — ⭐ 1,636 · `Jupyter Notebook` · Library — This repository provides simplified, from-scratch implementations of various reinforcement learning algorithms. - [R6410418/Jackrong-llm-finetuning-guide](https://github.com/R6410418/Jackrong-llm-finetuning-guide) — ⭐ 1,421 · `Jupyter Notebook` · Tutorial/Course — A beginner-friendly guide covering various techniques for fine-tuning large language models (LLMs), including reinforcement learning (RL) workflows, using popular frameworks like Unsloth.
- [PrimeIntellect-ai/prime-rl](https://github.com/PrimeIntellect-ai/prime-rl) — ⭐ 1,363 · `Python` · Framework — This project focuses on large-scale agentic Reinforcement Learning (RL) training, capable of handling massive models across distributed GPU setups.
- [aiming-lab/Agent0](https://github.com/aiming-lab/Agent0) — ⭐ 1,212 · `Python` · Agent Framework — This repository provides a framework for training self-improving AI agents. - [upb-lea/reinforcement\_learning\_course\_materials](https://github.com/upb-lea/reinforcement_learning_course_materials) — ⭐ 1,088 · `Jupyter Notebook` · Tutorial/Course — A comprehensive collection of lecture notes, tutorial tasks with solutions, and online video lectures for a university-level course on reinforcement learning.
- [WooooDyy/AgentGym-RL](https://github.com/WooooDyy/AgentGym-RL) — ⭐ 735 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository contains the code and implementations from a research paper on training LLM agents. - [rlresearch/dr-tulu](https://github.com/rlresearch/dr-tulu) — ⭐ 645 · `Python` · Paper Code — DR Tulu is a research repository for Reinforcement Learning with Evolving Rubrics, designed for deep research applications. - [Gen-Verse/ReasonFlux](https://github.com/Gen-Verse/ReasonFlux) — ⭐ 540 · `Python` · Library — A post-training suite for Large Language Models (LLMs), featuring techniques like reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) and process reward models to improve LLM performance, specifically including ReasonFlux, ReasonFlux-PRM, and ReasonFlux-Coder.
- [A-EVO-Lab/a-evolve](https://github.com/A-EVO-Lab/a-evolve) — ⭐ 530 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository showcases research on "Agentic Evolution," a concept for evolving LLMs to enhance their capabilities. - [TextArena/TextArena](https://github.com/TextArena/TextArena) — ⭐ 408 · `Python` · Framework — TextArena is a collection of competitive text-based games designed to serve as environments for language model evaluation and reinforcement learning research.
- [ECNU-ICALK/AutoSkill](https://github.com/ECNU-ICALK/AutoSkill) — ⭐ 398 · `Python` · Agent Framework — AutoSkill enables experience-driven lifelong learning for AI agents, allowing them to self-evolve and extract reusable skills from their interactions.
- [ltjed/multiagent-coaching](https://github.com/ltjed/multiagent-coaching) — ⭐ 328 · `Python` · Library — This project offers a method called MAPPA to fine-tune any multi-agent system on any task. It achieves this by employing per-action AI feedback, enabling continual learning for multi-agent reinforcement learning.
- [vllm-project/vime](https://github.com/vllm-project/vime) — ⭐ 238 · `Python` · Framework — Vime is an LLM post-training framework designed for Reinforcement Learning (RL) scaling scenarios, integrating with vLLM to serve as a rollout backend.
- [benchflow-ai/benchflow](https://github.com/benchflow-ai/benchflow) — ⭐ 228 · `Python` · Framework — Benchflow is a framework designed for creating high-fidelity and complex Reinforcement Learning (RL) environments and evaluation tasks, including the evaluation of AI skills.
- [Tencent-Hunyuan/UniRL](https://github.com/Tencent-Hunyuan/UniRL) — ⭐ 191 · `Python` · Framework — A framework for applying and developing reinforcement learning algorithms specifically designed for unified multimodal models, including LLMs, VLMs, and diffusion/flow matching models. - [Danau5tin/ai-trains-ai](https://github.com/Danau5tin/ai-trains-ai) — ⭐ 190 · `Python` · Paper Code — This project explores a two-layer Reinforcement Learning (RL) system where a primary AI agent is trained to automatically generate and manage RL training tasks for other, smaller AI models, aiming to automate and optimize model training.
- [alxndrTL/little-book-rl](https://github.com/alxndrTL/little-book-rl) — ⭐ 175 · `Python` · Tutorial/Course — A concise book on Reinforcement Learning, provided with supplementary materials for community use and deeper understanding.
- [hexo-ai/sia](https://github.com/hexo-ai/sia) — ⭐ 164 · `Python` · Framework — SIA is a Self Improving AI framework designed to autonomously enhance the performance of any AI system or agent on specific benchmark tasks.

- [Enping-Hu/minimind-deep-dive](https://github.com/Enping-Hu/minimind-deep-dive) — ⭐ 101 · `Python` · Tutorial/Course — This is a study guide and learning notes in Chinese that deeply analyzes the MiniMind source code and extends to modern large language model (LLM) technology. It covers pretraining, SFT, DPO, PPO, GRPO, quantization, speculative decoding, and RLHF, helping readers understand the underlying mechanisms.

It offers a simple and effective approach for developing more capable AI agents in search tasks.
- [adithya-s-k/RL\_Envs\_101](https://github.com/adithya-s-k/RL_Envs_101) — ⭐ 74 · `Python` · Framework — This repository focuses on the challenges and techniques for building and scaling Reinforcement Learning (RL) environments, particularly in the context of integrating with LLMs.
- [ScalingIntelligence/TRACE](https://github.com/ScalingIntelligence/TRACE) — ⭐ 73 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository provides the code for TRACE, a method focused on Capability-Targeted Agentic Training, including components related to Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) composition. It aims to develop and understand advanced training techniques for AI agents.
- [shawn0728/OpenSearch-VL](https://github.com/shawn0728/OpenSearch-VL) — ⭐ 60 · `Python` · Framework — OpenSearch-VL provides a comprehensive recipe for training powerful multimodal deep search agents. - [open-compass/AgentCompass](https://github.com/open-compass/AgentCompass) — ⭐ 54 · `Python` · Framework — AgentCompass is an extensible open-source infrastructure designed for systematically evaluating the capabilities of LLM and VLM agents.

- [microsoft/echo-rl](https://github.com/microsoft/echo-rl) — ⭐ 53 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository contains code and resources accompanying a Microsoft research paper on Reinforcement Learning, likely providing implementations or experimental setups.
- [Yonkoo11/hermes-dojo](https://github.com/Yonkoo11/hermes-dojo) — ⭐ 53 · `Python` · Framework — This system offers a self-improvement framework for the Hermes Agent. It monitors agent performance, identifies weak skills, and facilitates self-evolution to improve them, reporting on progress.
- [AMAP-ML/APPO](https://github.com/AMAP-ML/APPO) — ⭐ 47 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository provides the code implementation for 'Asynchronous Proximal Policy Optimization (APPO)', a deep reinforcement learning method. It is associated with a research paper.
- [XIAO4579/PRISM](https://github.com/XIAO4579/PRISM) — ⭐ 43 · `Python` · Paper Code — PRISM introduces a pre-alignment method for Multimodal Reinforcement Learning (RL) using black-box on-policy distillation. It enhances multimodal models, offering an alternative to standard SFT-to-RL approaches.
- [JacckMa/pipo\_verl](https://github.com/JacckMa/pipo_verl) — ⭐ 42 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository introduces PIPO, a method that allows AI models to verify their own improvement before updates. It operates by checking each update against past performance to reinforce gains and suppress regressions, resulting in consistent performance improvements over PPO.
- [RL-Align/RL-Kernel](https://github.com/RL-Align/RL-Kernel) — ⭐ 42 · `Python` · Library — RL-Kernel is a modern post-training infrastructure optimized for NVIDIA/AMD GPUs, focusing on vLLM integration, Triton kernels, and transparent hardware-aware scaling, designed for RLHF training of LLMs.
- [hongruhou89/ProRL](https://github.com/hongruhou89/ProRL) — ⭐ 35 · `Python` · Paper Code — ProRL implements an effective reinforcement learning approach for proactive recommendation systems. It uses rectified policy gradient estimation to outperform existing baselines on real-world datasets and generalizes to new recommenders.
- [Sphere-AI-Lab/orbit](https://github.com/Sphere-AI-Lab/orbit) — ⭐ 33 · `Python` · Framework — Orbit is an OFT-based reinforcement learning infrastructure that provides stable and efficient post-training capabilities for trillion-parameter LLMs. It allows for the training of extremely large language models on a single GPU node with high efficiency and a small train-rollout gap.
- [OSU-NLP-Group/ACuRL](https://github.com/OSU-NLP-Group/ACuRL) — ⭐ 31 · `Python` · Framework — This repository provides ACuRL, an Autonomous Curriculum Reinforcement Learning framework designed for agents to continuously learn in various environments without requiring human data. It offers a full RL infrastructure for Curriculum-guided Unsupervised Agents and is accompanied by a research paper.
- [zli12321/LHTB](https://github.com/zli12321/LHTB) — ⭐ 27 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository offers a Long Horizon Terminal Benchmark with dense reward grading, likely serving as a tool for evaluating AI agents, particularly in the context of reinforcement learning research.
- [MindLab-Research/mindlab-toolkit](https://github.com/MindLab-Research/mindlab-toolkit) — ⭐ 27 · `Python` · Library — This is a Python toolkit designed for MinT, an open infrastructure focused on experiential intelligence and LoRA Reinforcement Learning (RL). It provides the necessary tools and utilities for working with this specific AI infrastructure.
- [perplexityai/wandr](https://github.com/perplexityai/wandr) — ⭐ 27 · `Python` · Framework — An evaluation and reinforcement learning environment designed to measure and improve the performance of agentic search systems. It is synthesized from real-world production traces to provide a realistic distribution for training capable models.
- [uynitsuj/WARP-RM](https://github.com/uynitsuj/WARP-RM) — ⭐ 27 · `Python` · Paper Code — WARP-RM is a Warp-Augmented Relative Progress Reward Model, offering training code, checkpoints, an evaluation suite, and metric definitions for data curation within simulated environments.
- [Peregrine123/ROPD\_official](https://github.com/Peregrine123/ROPD_official) — ⭐ 26 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository contains the official code implementation for the ROPD (Rubric-based On-Policy Distillation) method. ROPD generates prompt-specific rubrics using teacher-student model contrasts to score rollouts for on-policy optimization, allowing compatibility with any black-box teacher model.
- [sharifnassab/Intentional\_RL](https://github.com/sharifnassab/Intentional_RL) — ⭐ 23 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository implements "intentional updates," a novel approach for streaming reinforcement learning that addresses instability issues encountered with standard step sizes when learning from continuous experience streams. It proposes a method to optimize learning by first determining the desired update rather than relying on fixed step sizes.
- [OSU-NLP-Group/QUEST](https://github.com/OSU-NLP-Group/QUEST) — ⭐ 18 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository introduces Quest, an open family of deep research agents (2B–35B) trained using a synthetic data pipeline. It details a mid-training, Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT), and Reinforcement Learning (RL) recipe for developing these frontier agents.
- [Spreadsheet-RL/Spreadsheet-RL](https://github.com/Spreadsheet-RL/Spreadsheet-RL) — ⭐ 18 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository provides the official code for Spreadsheet-RL, a project from Meta and UIUC focusing on LLM spreadsheet agents. It includes the full stack for developing these agents, encompassing data, environment, verifier/reward functions, and the reinforcement learning pipeline.
- [machinestein/Zero-Shot-Off-Policy-Learning](https://github.com/machinestein/Zero-Shot-Off-Policy-Learning) — ⭐ 16 · `Jupyter Notebook` · Paper Code — This repository contains the official PyTorch implementation for the research paper "Zero-Shot Off-Policy Learning." It explores advanced reinforcement learning techniques focused on enabling models to perform tasks without prior direct experience in the target environment.
- [machinestein/Deep-Improvement-Supervision](https://github.com/machinestein/Deep-Improvement-Supervision) — ⭐ 14 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository contains the official PyTorch implementation for a research paper that posits latent reasoning in Tiny Recursive Models (TRMs) functions as a policy improvement operator. It delves into hierarchical reasoning and discrete diffusion within the context of reinforcement learning research.
- [T-Lab-CUHKSZ/G2RPO-A](https://github.com/T-Lab-CUHKSZ/G2RPO-A) — ⭐ 14 · `Python` · Paper Code — G²RPO-A (Guided Group Relative Policy Optimization with Adaptive Guidance) is a research contribution aimed at improving the reasoning capabilities of smaller language models. It adaptively feeds correct reasoning steps into the training process, dynamically adjusting guidance as the model improves, and performs well on math and code benchmarks.
- [thu-rllab/GPS](https://github.com/thu-rllab/GPS) — ⭐ 11 · `Python` · Paper Code — GPS is a lightweight predictive model that employs Bayesian inference to estimate prompt difficulty, enabling more efficient RL post-training for large reasoning models by intelligently selecting prompts. This research aims to reduce the costs and time associated with model training.
- [huggingface/Repo2RLEnv](https://github.com/huggingface/Repo2RLEnv) — ⭐ 10 · `Python` · Framework — This project allows users to convert any GitHub repository into a Reinforcement Learning environment, facilitating the training of AI agents on code-related tasks.

</details>

## Distributed Training

Shard, parallelize, and scale across hundreds of GPUs without melting your budget

- [hpcaitech/ColossalAI](https://github.com/hpcaitech/ColossalAI) — ⭐ 41,383 · `Python` · Framework — This project provides a comprehensive framework and tools designed to make training and deploying large AI models more efficient, cheaper, and accessible. It leverages distributed computing techniques like data, model, and pipeline parallelism to accelerate performance and reduce resource requirements for big models.
- [transformerlab/transformerlab-app](https://github.com/transformerlab/transformerlab-app) — ⭐ 4,946 · `Python` · Application — An open-source research environment for AI researchers to seamlessly train, evaluate, and scale models from local hardware to GPU clusters.
- [learning-at-home/hivemind](https://github.com/learning-at-home/hivemind) — ⭐ 2,442 · `Python` · Framework — This repository provides a PyTorch-based framework for decentralized deep learning. It enables training large models across thousands of volunteer computers globally, utilizing distributed systems and asynchronous programming.
- [mlc-ai/pith-train](https://github.com/mlc-ai/pith-train) — ⭐ 92 · `Python` · Framework — Pith is a compact and agent-native training system designed for Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models, aiming to simplify framework setup and enable easy experimentation with new architectures on ML clusters.
- [PaddlePaddle/PaddleFleet](https://github.com/PaddlePaddle/PaddleFleet) — ⭐ 22 · `Python` · Library — PaddleFleet is a core functional Python library designed for distributed training, particularly focused on mitigating loss spikes and improving optimizer stability in large-scale LLM pretraining.
- [MindLab-Research/longstraw](https://github.com/MindLab-Research/longstraw) — ⭐ 10 · `Python` · Framework — LongStraw is a long-context training system for large language models, employing techniques like resident-prefix GRPO to optimize memory usage by efficiently processing ultra-long prompts and trading time for memory during gradient computation, supporting distributed training for very long sequence lengths.

## Distillation

Compress a giant teacher into a tiny student that punches way above its size

- [apple/ml-ssd](https://github.com/apple/ml-ssd) — ⭐ 764 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository provides the code implementation for a self-distillation method in machine learning. It accompanies a research paper and focuses on the simplicity and underlying understanding of the technique for model training.
- [thunlp/OPD](https://github.com/thunlp/OPD) — ⭐ 355 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository contains the code for the research paper "Rethinking On-Policy Distillation of Large Language Models", exploring the phenomena, mechanisms, and recipes for distilling LLMs using an on-policy approach.
- [kokolerk/TCOD](https://github.com/kokolerk/TCOD) — ⭐ 55 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository provides the code and resources for a research paper exploring Temporal Curriculum in On-Policy Distillation, a method designed to enhance the training efficiency and effectiveness of multi-turn autonomous agents.
- [vvvvvjdy/D-OPSD](https://github.com/vvvvvjdy/D-OPSD) — ⭐ 31 · Paper Code — This is the official repository for the paper "D-OPSD: On-Policy Self-Distillation for Continuously Tuning Step-Distilled Diffusion Models."
- [TsinghuaC3I/ZEDA](https://github.com/TsinghuaC3I/ZEDA) — ⭐ 15 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository introduces ZEDA, a lightweight method for optimizing post-trained Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models. It allows MoEs to dynamically skip up to half of their experts during inference, significantly reducing computational cost and improving inference speed for both easy and hard tokens.
- [xingzhejun/d-opsd-code](https://github.com/xingzhejun/d-opsd-code) — ⭐ 14 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository contains the reference code for a research paper on "On-policy Self-distillation for dLLMs," a novel framework enabling diffusion LLMs to learn from their own future answers.
- [sunjie279/SimCT-](https://github.com/sunjie279/SimCT-) — ⭐ 13 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository contains the code implementation for a research paper on "Cross-tokenizer OPD" (Optimal Policy Distillation), focusing on improving supervision interfaces for training.
- [Qwen-Applications/CollectionLoRA](https://github.com/Qwen-Applications/CollectionLoRA) — ⭐ 11 · `Python` · Paper Code — CollectionLoRA implements a method to combine 50 different effects into a single LoRA model using multi-teacher on-policy distillation.

## Quantization

Squeeze models to 4-bit and below while keeping the accuracy that matters

<details>
<summary>Show 13 repos</summary>

- [TheTom/turboquant\_plus](https://github.com/TheTom/turboquant_plus) — ⭐ 6,860 · `Python` · Library — TurboQuant+ is an optimization for KV cache memory within the DSV4-Flash inference engine, achieving 4.75x memory reduction using 3-bit quantization. It supports CUDA and Metal, maintaining high accuracy compared to FP8.
- [openai/parameter-golf](https://github.com/openai/parameter-golf) — ⭐ 5,067 · `Python` · Application — This repository presents a challenge, known as "parameter golf," where participants compete to train the smallest possible language model that adheres to a strict 16MB memory constraint.
- [vllm-project/llm-compressor](https://github.com/vllm-project/llm-compressor) — ⭐ 3,223 · `Python` · Library — A Python library designed to compress large language models using various algorithms, enabling optimized and efficient deployment with vLLM.
- [NVIDIA/Model-Optimizer](https://github.com/NVIDIA/Model-Optimizer) — ⭐ 2,695 · `Python` · Library — This is a unified library providing state-of-the-art deep learning model optimization techniques such as quantization, pruning, distillation, and speculative decoding. It's designed to compress deep learning models for faster inference when deployed with frameworks like TensorRT-LLM and vLLM.
- [Tencent/AngelSlim](https://github.com/Tencent/AngelSlim) — ⭐ 1,426 · `Python` · Library — AngelSlim is a Python-based toolkit from Tencent for comprehensive and efficient model compression. It focuses on techniques like quantization and speculative decoding to significantly reduce the size and memory footprint of large language and vision models (LLMs, VLMs) with minimal performance degradation.
- [intel/auto-round](https://github.com/intel/auto-round) — ⭐ 1,401 · `Python` · Paper Code — AutoRound is a state-of-the-art quantization algorithm for high-accuracy, low-bit inference of Large Language Models, optimized for various hardware (CPU/XPU/CUDA) and fully compatible with popular LLM serving frameworks like vLLM, SGLang, and Transformers.
- [adamzweiger/compaction](https://github.com/adamzweiger/compaction) — ⭐ 237 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository provides algorithms for latent compaction, a technique to significantly reduce the memory footprint of language models by building compact key-value caches in latent space, thereby improving efficiency during inference.
- [Dynamis-Labs/spectralquant](https://github.com/Dynamis-Labs/spectralquant) — ⭐ 176 · `Python` · Paper Code — SpectralQuant is a research-oriented repository presenting methods for KV-cache compression in large language models and transformers. It uses calibrated eigenbasis rotation and water-filled bit allocation to reduce memory footprint and accelerate LLM inference.
- [SparkEngineAI/QuantClaw-plugin](https://github.com/SparkEngineAI/QuantClaw-plugin) — ⭐ 107 · `TypeScript` · Plugin/Extension — QuantClaw is a plug-and-play plugin for OpenClaw that dynamically quantizes large language models. It enables AI agents to select optimal precision levels for tasks, thereby optimizing both cost and performance.
- [FutureMLS-Lab/OSCAR](https://github.com/FutureMLS-Lab/OSCAR) — ⭐ 74 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository contains the official code for OSCAR, a method for offline spectral covariance-aware rotation used to quantize KV caches to 2-bit precision, aimed at optimizing the memory and inference efficiency of large language models.
- [zhangsq-nju/EdgeRazor](https://github.com/zhangsq-nju/EdgeRazor) — ⭐ 61 · `Python` · Framework — EdgeRazor is a lightweight Python framework designed for optimizing Large Language Models. It uses mixed-precision quantization-aware distillation to reduce model size and enable efficient deployment on edge devices like mobile phones, without significant loss in performance.
- [silveroxides/convert\_to\_quant](https://github.com/silveroxides/convert_to_quant) — ⭐ 53 · `Python` · CLI Tool — This tool allows users to convert model weights (safetensors) into various quantized formats like FP8, INT8, NVFP4, and MixedFP8. It is designed to reduce VRAM usage during inference in tools like ComfyUI, with minimal quality degradation.
- [ZunhaiSu/OScaR-KV-Quant](https://github.com/ZunhaiSu/OScaR-KV-Quant) — ⭐ 17 · `C++` · Paper Code — This repository provides OScaR, a novel method for extreme KV cache quantization in Large Language Models (LLMs) and other X-LLMs, aiming to significantly improve both accuracy and efficiency.

</details>

## Pretraining

Build foundation models from scratch with battle-tested training stacks

<details>
<summary>Show 12 repos</summary>

- [jingyaogong/minimind](https://github.com/jingyaogong/minimind) — ⭐ 53,166 · `Python` · Tutorial/Course — This project provides a complete codebase and guide for training a small-parameter Large Language Model (LLM) from scratch, covering tokenization, pre-training, and fine-tuning. It also demonstrates support for MoE architecture and includes a multimodal variant, MiniMind-V, illustrating the full LLM development pipeline.
- [seal-rg/recurrent-pretraining](https://github.com/seal-rg/recurrent-pretraining) — ⭐ 898 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository provides the code for pretraining and running inference on a large-scale depth-recurrent language model. It focuses on developing and testing advanced LLM architectures for improved reasoning capabilities.
- [FareedKhan-dev/train-llm-from-scratch](https://github.com/FareedKhan-dev/train-llm-from-scratch) — ⭐ 585 · `Jupyter Notebook` · Tutorial/Course — This repository provides a straightforward method for training Large Language Models (LLMs) from scratch, covering everything from data downloading to text generation, specifically designed to be achievable on a single GPU.
- [qlabs-eng/slowrun](https://github.com/qlabs-eng/slowrun) — ⭐ 485 · `Python` · Paper Code — A research project demonstrating a novel training methodology for Large Language Models using recursive/looped architectures. It aims to achieve superior validation loss compared to state-of-the-art baselines under iso-FLOP and iso-Param constraints.
- [raiyanyahya/how-to-train-your-gpt](https://github.com/raiyanyahya/how-to-train-your-gpt) — ⭐ 428 · `Jupyter Notebook` · Tutorial/Course — This repository provides a tutorial that guides users through building a modern Large Language Model (LLM) from scratch, with thoroughly commented code and simplified explanations.
- [Doriandarko/texts-to-transformer](https://github.com/Doriandarko/texts-to-transformer) — ⭐ 326 · `Python` · Application — This repository allows users to train a small Transformer model from scratch using their iMessage history, designed to run entirely on a Mac. It provides a local-first solution for personal LLM training.
- [NatLabRockies/BuildingsBench](https://github.com/NatLabRockies/BuildingsBench) — ⭐ 93 · `Jupyter Notebook` · Paper Code — This project provides a framework for large-scale pretraining and benchmarking of models for short-term load forecasting, utilizing a nationwide building dataset to evaluate predictive performance.
- [HaiyuWu/visreg](https://github.com/HaiyuWu/visreg) — ⭐ 92 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository implements VISReg, a novel regularization-based method for Self-Supervised Learning (SSL). It aims to simplify the training heuristics of current SSL methods while achieving comparable or superior generalization performance.
- [soran-ghaderi/torchebm](https://github.com/soran-ghaderi/torchebm) — ⭐ 91 · `Python` · Library — This library provides tools and functionalities in PyTorch for building and training energy-based models and diffusion models. It simplifies the development of generative AI models, offering various sampling and inference methods.
- [The-School-of-AI/LLM](https://github.com/The-School-of-AI/LLM) — ⭐ 62 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository provides LightningLM, a reference training pipeline for developing large language models, demonstrating state-preserving scaling from a 2B dense model to a 120B sparse Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) model. It utilizes techniques like TurboQuant-PreTraining and serves as companion code for the 'Reversible Foundations' research paper.
- [walkinglabs/modern-llm-notebook](https://github.com/walkinglabs/modern-llm-notebook) — ⭐ 41 · `Jupyter Notebook` · Tutorial/Course — This repository presents a comprehensive, hands-on course comprising 23 Jupyter Notebooks, guiding users to build modern large language models (LLMs) from scratch in PyTorch. It covers fundamental and advanced topics such as tokenizers, attention mechanisms, Mixture of Experts (MoE), Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF), inference, evaluation, and model distillation.
- [gszfwsb/OPUS](https://github.com/gszfwsb/OPUS) — ⭐ 11 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository contains the official code for the ICML 2026 paper "OPUS", focusing on an efficient and principled approach to data selection for large language model pre-training. It aims to optimize the data used in each pre-training iteration to improve model development.

</details>

It also includes research to derive the first scaling laws for these models, focusing on stable and hassle-free training.
- [DeepTrackAI/DeepLearningCrashCourse](https://github.com/DeepTrackAI/DeepLearningCrashCourse) — ⭐ 146 · `Jupyter Notebook` · Tutorial/Course — This "Deep Learning Crash Course" is a comprehensive and up-to-date guide that teaches deep learning architectures, from simple neural networks to cutting-edge models, with hands-on projects covering CNNs, GANs, and Transformers.
- [wq-will/SimpleTES](https://github.com/wq-will/SimpleTES) — ⭐ 125 · `C++` · Framework — This is a general framework for strategically scaling evaluation-driven discovery loops, which helps in finding state-of-the-art solutions for various open-ended problems.
- [LeapLabTHU/ViTTT](https://github.com/LeapLabTHU/ViTTT) — ⭐ 124 · `Python` · Paper Code — This is the official repository for ViT^3, a Vision Test-Time Training architecture presented at CVPR 2026. It enables vision models to adapt and train themselves on the fly, creating an online learning loop within each layer without requiring extra data or labels.
- [LoganBooker/prodigy-plus-schedule-free](https://github.com/LoganBooker/prodigy-plus-schedule-free) — ⭐ 94 · `Python` · Library — This repository integrates Schedule-Free Optimization with Prodigy, an adaptive optimization algorithm, to enhance the efficiency and performance of deep learning model training. It provides a combined approach in PyTorch for faster and more stable experiment iteration.
- [SingleZombie/LLSA](https://github.com/SingleZombie/LLSA) — ⭐ 78 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository provides the official implementation of Log-linear Sparse Attention (LLSA), a deep learning attention mechanism highlighted at CVPR 2026.
- [Optim-Agent/optim-agent](https://github.com/Optim-Agent/optim-agent) — ⭐ 67 · `Python` · AI Agent — This repository provides autonomous LLM agents designed to optimize hyperparameters for machine learning training runs, aiming to reduce computational waste from brute-force search methods.
- [deep-spin/adasplash](https://github.com/deep-spin/adasplash) — ⭐ 42 · `Python` · Library — AdaSplash provides an implementation of Adaptive Sparse Flash Attention, also known as Flash Entmax Attention, including optimized Triton kernels for efficient model training.
- [ZonglinY/MOOSE-Star](https://github.com/ZonglinY/MOOSE-Star) — ⭐ 32 · `Python` · Paper Code — This project, MOOSE-Star, introduces a novel method to enable tractable training of Large Language Models (LLMs) specifically for scientific discovery, aiming to overcome complexity barriers in generating scientific hypotheses.
- [Sphere-AI-Lab/pion](https://github.com/Sphere-AI-Lab/pion) — ⭐ 30 · `Python` · Library — Pion is a novel optimizer designed to enhance the stability of Large Language Model (LLM) training without compromising performance. Developed by researchers, it employs a unique method of rotating each weight matrix during optimization, distinguishing it from traditional optimizers.
- [MagellaX/StreamAttn](https://github.com/MagellaX/StreamAttn) — ⭐ 29 · `Python` · Library — This repository provides a high-performance attention mechanism that implements an online softmax computation, allowing for softmax normalization in a single streaming pass using running accumulators. It is particularly relevant for LLMs and RL applications.
- [GT-KOALA/FFOLayer](https://github.com/GT-KOALA/FFOLayer) — ⭐ 22 · `Jupyter Notebook` · Library — FFOLayer introduces a novel fully first-order differentiable optimization layer for machine learning models, simplifying the backward pass in optimization. This component was presented as a paper at ICML2026.
- [LMMMEng/CaRE](https://github.com/LMMMEng/CaRE) — ⭐ 19 · `Python` · Paper Code — This project, CaRE, proposes a continual learning system utilizing Bi-Level Routing Mixture-of-Experts to handle over 300 tasks efficiently. It aims to prevent catastrophic forgetting in AI by dynamically routing to task-specific experts rather than retraining the entire model.
- [SakanaAI/DiffusionBlocks](https://github.com/SakanaAI/DiffusionBlocks) — ⭐ 19 · `Python` · Library — This repository introduces DiffusionBlocks, a method for block-wise neural network training via diffusion interpretation. It aims to reduce memory requirements during the training of large neural networks.
- [denghuilin-cyber/IIB-LPO](https://github.com/denghuilin-cyber/IIB-LPO) — ⭐ 19 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository contains the code for I²B-LPO, a method developed by Alibaba's DAMO Academy and partners to improve AI model reasoning. It addresses narrow reasoning by branching at confusion points and using an information bottleneck for efficient processing.
- [mll-lab-nu/BAGEN](https://github.com/mll-lab-nu/BAGEN) — ⭐ 12 · `Python` · Framework — BAGEN is a Python framework designed for evaluating, training, and understanding AI agents that are "budget-aware." These agents learn to predict remaining budget intervals and proactively alert users to prevent overspending on tasks.
- [Mog9/research-papers](https://github.com/Mog9/research-papers) — ⭐ 12 · `Python` · Library — This repository contains research implementations primarily focused on improving inference efficiency and optimizing AI models. It includes techniques like custom Triton kernels, LoRA, and knowledge distillation pipelines.
- [locuslab/EqR](https://github.com/locuslab/EqR) — ⭐ 10 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository is associated with a research paper presented at ICML 2026, focusing on how test-time computation and inner model dynamics contribute to a model's intelligence and convergence. It likely contains code to reproduce or explore these research findings.

</details>
